message:
fix(vacuum): use original DDL SQL instead of reconstructing it

VACUUM's persist step was regenerating CREATE TABLE SQL from in-memory
schema metadata via build_create_table_sql(). This lossy reconstruction
could:
- Drop column-level CHECK constraints (ColumnInfo has no field for them)
- Alter DEFAULT value formatting (relies on Expr::to_string())
- Emit constraints in wrong order (fixed in previous commit, but fragile)

Now persist_to_sqlite_with_header_and_master_entries accepts an optional
original_ddl map (HashMap<String, String> keyed by object name) and
prefers it over build_create_table_sql when available. The VACUUM path
passes self.original_ddl_sql, which holds the exact CREATE TABLE SQL
as written by the user. Non-VACUUM callers pass an empty map, falling
back to reconstruction (safe for in-memory schemas without prior DDL).

This eliminates an entire class of VACUUM schema fidelity bugs and is
a net performance win (HashMap lookup vs string formatting + allocation).

Added regression test verifying column-level CHECK constraints
survive VACUUM and remain enforceable by stock SQLite.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
